news.title: Drukke dagen tijdens WK voetbal zorgen nauwelijks voor hogere omzet in horeca - NU
news.summary:
# Summary

Despite busy crowds during the World Cup football tournament, Dutch restaurants and bars (horeca) saw surprisingly little increase in revenue. The article doesn't mention a specific restaurant but discusses the hospitality sector broadly in the Netherlands, suggesting that while venues were crowded with football fans, this didn't translate into significantly higher sales. Visitors to the Netherlands during major sporting events might be surprised to learn that popular dining spots may be packed but not necessarily more profitable or offering better service due to staffing constraints.

news.title: De Vrouw met de Baard opent in restaurant van Pakhuis de Zwijger: ‘Wat veel mensen niet weten: rendang is een bereidingswijze, geen gerecht’ - Het Parool
news.summary:
# Summary

De Vrouw met de Baard, a Dutch food concept, has opened a new restaurant location inside Pakhuis de Zwijger in Amsterdam. The restaurant focuses on Indonesian cuisine and highlights lesser-known culinary facts, such as that rendang is a cooking technique rather than a specific dish. Visitors to Amsterdam should note this spot for authentic, educational Indonesian dining in a cultural venue setting.

news.title: Ze werkte voor de bekendste chefs van Antwerpen, nu start Senna (30) het eigenzinnige BOE: “Het lijkt op een theehuis in Hongkong” - GVA
news.summary:
# Summary

Senna, a 30-year-old chef who previously worked under Antwerp's most renowned chefs, has opened a new restaurant called BOE with a distinctive concept inspired by a Hong Kong teahouse. This unique dining spot offers visitors to Antwerp something different from the city's traditional restaurant scene. The restaurant represents an exciting addition for food lovers seeking innovative and unconventional culinary experiences in the city.

news.summary:
# Summary

The owners of two popular Antwerp bars—Bar du Port and 't Licht der Dokken—have taken over management of De Lindekens, marking their first café location outside the city center. This is noteworthy for visitors because it brings the established quality and reputation of these well-regarded downtown bars to a new neighborhood spot. If you enjoy the vibe of these successful urban venues, you now have another option to experience their style in a different setting.
